mnemonics: Make Pictures Meaningful: Label, Annotate, Title
keyPoints: Matplotlib's pyplot interface provides a MATLAB-like way to create plots.
The basic plot creation flow is: create figure/axes -> plot data -> customize plot -> show/save plot.
Understanding the difference between the object-oriented interface and the pyplot state-machine interface is crucial for advanced plotting.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call plt.show() to display the plot, especially in non-interactive environments.
Overplotting by using default styles and colors, making lines hard to distinguish.
Not setting appropriate limits (xlim, ylim) or labels (xlabel, ylabel, title), resulting in an unclear visualization.
